Title.FullName: United Kingdom General Data Protection Regulation

Preamble.ConstitutionalAuthority: Derived from EU Regulation 2016/679 as retained under Section 3 of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 and amended by the Data Protection Act 2018.
Preamble.PurposeAndObjectives: To safeguard the fundamental rights and freedoms of natural persons, particularly the right to the protection of personal data, while ensuring the free flow of personal data within the United Kingdom.
Preamble.ContextualJustification: Ensures continuity of data protection law post-Brexit, maintaining GDPR-level protections while enabling the UK to independently adapt data protection policy.

Scope.TerritorialScope: Applies to processing of personal data in the context of an establishment of a controller or processor in the UK, and to entities outside the UK offering goods or services to, or monitoring the behavior of, individuals in the UK.
Scope.MaterialScope: Applies to automated and non-automated structured processing of personal data, with exemptions for purely personal/household activities, national security, and processing under Part 3 (Law Enforcement) and Part 4 (Intelligence Services) of the Data Protection Act 2018.

KeyTerms.PersonalData: Information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person ('data subject').
KeyTerms.Controller: The natural or legal person, public authority, agency, or other body which determines the purposes and means of the processing of personal data.
KeyTerms.Processor: A natural or legal person, public authority, agency, or other body which processes personal data on behalf of the controller.
KeyTerms.DataSubject: An identified or identifiable natural person whose personal data are processed.
KeyTerms.Pseudonymisation: Processing of personal data so it can no longer be attributed to a specific data subject without additional information.
KeyTerms.FilingSystem: Any structured set of personal data accessible by specific criteria.

Definitions.InterpretiveProvisions: Terms referencing EU institutions are to be interpreted as UK equivalents; 'Commission' becomes the 'Secretary of State' where appropriate.

LawfulBases: Vital interests of the data subject
Public interest or exercise of official authority
Legitimate interests of the controller or third party
LawfulProcessing.ConditionsForConsent: Consent must be freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous. Parental consent is required for processing personal data of children under 13 for online services.
LawfulProcessing.ProcessingSpecialCategories: Prohibited unless one of the conditions in Article 9(2) is met, such as explicit consent or substantial public interest under UK law.
LawfulProcessing.CriminalOffenceData: Processing of criminal conviction/offence data requires official authority or conditions under Schedule 1 of the Data Protection Act 2018.

DataSubjectRights.Description: Transparency obligations on controllers to provide clear, concise, and accessible information to data subjects.
Right to obtain confirmation and access to personal data, including the purposes of processing and categories of data processed.
Right to have inaccurate personal data corrected without undue delay.
Right to request deletion of personal data in certain circumstances (e.g., withdrawal of consent, unlawful processing).
Right to limit processing in specified circumstances such as contested accuracy or pending objection outcome.
Right to receive personal data in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format and transmit it to another controller.
Right to object to processing based on public interest, legitimate interests, or direct marketing.
Right not to be subject to decisions based solely on automated processing, including profiling, with legal or significant effects.

SpecialDataCategories.Definition: Personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, genetic data, biometric data, health data, and sex life or sexual orientation.
ProcessingConditions: Employment, social security, and social protection law compliance
Substantial public interest under UK law

ObligationsOfControllersAndProcessors.GeneralDuties: Implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure compliance with the regulation.
ObligationsOfControllersAndProcessors.Accountability: Maintain records of processing activities and demonstrate compliance.
ObligationsOfControllersAndProcessors.DataProtectionOfficer: Mandatory for public authorities and organizations carrying out large-scale systematic monitoring or processing of sensitive data.
ObligationsOfControllersAndProcessors.SecurityMeasures: Implement measures such as pseudonymization, encryption, and resilience testing (Article 32).
ObligationsOfControllersAndProcessors.BreachNotification: Notify the ICO within 72 hours of discovering a personal data breach (Article 33).
ObligationsOfControllersAndProcessors.Processor Obligations: Controllers must ensure processors provide sufficient guarantees via written contracts.

CrossBorderTransfers.AdequacyFramework: Transfers permitted to countries deemed adequate by the UK Secretary of State.
CrossBorderTransfers.StandardContractualClauses: Transfers based on approved UK standard clauses.
CrossBorderTransfers.BindingCorporateRules: Permitted with ICO approval for intra-group transfers.
CrossBorderTransfers.Derogations: Transfers under explicit consent or for compelling legitimate interests when no adequacy or safeguards exist.

SupervisoryAuthority.Establishment: The Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO) is the UK supervisory authority.
SupervisoryAuthority.AdministrativeSanctions: Fines up to £17.5 million or 4% of global annual turnover, whichever is higher.

RemediesAndEnforcement.JudicialRemedies: Data subjects may seek judicial review of ICO decisions.
RemediesAndEnforcement.RightToCompensation: Data subjects have a right to compensation for material or non-material damage caused by violations.
RemediesAndEnforcement.Penalties: Administrative fines and criminal penalties under the Data Protection Act 2018.

FinalProvisions.ReviewAndAmendment: The Secretary of State may make amendments via regulations subject to Parliamentary approval.
